001// Copyright (c) Choreo contributors
002
003package choreo.auto;
004
005import static edu.wpi.first.wpilibj.Alert.AlertType.kError;
006
007import choreo.util.ChoreoAlert;
008import edu.wpi.first.util.sendable.Sendable;
009import edu.wpi.first.util.sendable.SendableBuilder;
010import edu.wpi.first.wpilibj.Alert;
011import edu.wpi.first.wpilibj.DriverStation;
012import edu.wpi.first.wpilibj2.command.Command;
013import edu.wpi.first.wpilibj2.command.Commands;
014import java.util.HashMap;
015import java.util.Map;
016import java.util.Set;
017import java.util.function.Supplier;
018
019/**
020 * An Choreo specific {@code SendableChooser} that allows for the selection of {@link AutoRoutine}s
021 * at runtime via a <a
022 * href="https://docs.wpilib.org/en/stable/docs/software/dashboards/index.html#dashboards">Dashboard</a>.
023 *
024 * <p>This chooser takes a <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lazy_loading">lazy loading</a>
025 * approach to {@link AutoRoutine}s, only generating the {@link AutoRoutine} when it is selected.
026 * This approach has the benefit of not loading all autos on startup, but also not loading the auto
027 * during auto start causing a delay.
028 *
029 * <p>Once the {@link AutoChooser} is made you can add {@link AutoRoutine}s to it using {@link
030 * #addRoutine} or add {@link Command}s to it using {@link #addCmd}. Similar to {@code
031 * SendableChooser} this chooser can be added to the {@link
032 * edu.wpi.first.wpilibj.smartdashboard.SmartDashboard} using {@code
033 * SmartDashboard.putData(Sendable)}.
034 *
035 * <p>You can set the Robot's autonomous command to the chooser's chosen auto routine via <code>
036 * RobotModeTriggers.autonomous.whileTrue(chooser.autoSchedulingCmd());</code>
037 */
038public class AutoChooser implements Sendable {
039  static final String NONE_NAME = "Nothing";
040  private static final Alert selectedNonexistentAuto =
041      ChoreoAlert.alert("Selected an auto that isn't an option", kError);
042
043  private final HashMap<String, Supplier<Command>> autoRoutines =
044      new HashMap<>(Map.of(NONE_NAME, Commands::none));
045
046  // private final StringEntry selected, active;
047  // private final StringArrayEntry options;
048  private String selected = NONE_NAME;
049  private String[] options = new String[] {NONE_NAME};
050
051  private String activeCommandName = NONE_NAME;
052  private Command selectedCommand = Commands.none();
053
054  /** Constructs a new {@link AutoChooser}. */
055  public AutoChooser() {}
056
057  /**
058   * Select a new option in the chooser.
059   *
060   * <p>This method is called automatically when published as a sendable.
061   *
062   * @param selectStr The name of the option to select.
063   * @return The name of the selected option.
064   */
065  public String select(String selectStr) {
066    selected = selectStr;
067    if (DriverStation.isDisabled()
068        && DriverStation.isDSAttached()
069        && DriverStation.getAlliance().isPresent()) {
070      if (selected.equals(activeCommandName)) return activeCommandName;
071      if (!autoRoutines.containsKey(selected) && !selected.equals(NONE_NAME)) {
072        selected = NONE_NAME;
073        selectedNonexistentAuto.set(true);
074      } else {
075        selectedNonexistentAuto.set(false);
076      }
077      activeCommandName = selected;
078    }
079    selectedCommand = autoRoutines.get(activeCommandName).get();
080    return activeCommandName;
081  }
082
083  /**
084   * Add an AutoRoutine to the chooser.
085   *
086   * <p>The options of the chooser are actually a function that takes an {@link AutoFactory} and
087   * returns a {@link AutoRoutine}. These functions can be static, a lambda or belong to a local
088   * variable.
089   *
090   * <p>This is done to load AutoRoutines when and only when they are selected, in order to save
091   * memory and file loading time for unused AutoRoutines.
092   *
093   * <p>One way to keep this clean is to make an `Autos` class that all of your subsystems/resources
094   * are <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dependency_injection">dependency injected</a> into.
095   * Then create methods inside that class that take an {@link AutoFactory} and return an {@link
096   * AutoRoutine}.
097   *
098   * <h3>Example:</h3>
099   *
100   * <pre><code>
101   * AutoChooser chooser;
102   * Autos autos = new Autos(swerve, shooter, intake, feeder);
103   * public Robot() {
104   *   chooser = new AutoChooser("/Choosers");
105   *   SmartDashboard.putData(chooser);
106   *   // fourPieceRight is a method that accepts an AutoFactory and returns an AutoRoutine.
107   *   chooser.addRoutine("4 Piece right", autos::fourPieceRight);
108   *   chooser.addRoutine("4 Piece Left", autos::fourPieceLeft);
109   *   chooser.addRoutine("3 Piece Close", autos::threePieceClose);
110   * }
111   * </code></pre>
112   *
113   * @param name The name of the auto routine.
114   * @param generator The function that generates the auto routine.
115   */
116  public void addRoutine(String name, Supplier<AutoRoutine> generator) {
117    autoRoutines.put(name, () -> generator.get().cmd());
118    options = autoRoutines.keySet().toArray(new String[0]);
119  }
120
121  /**
122   * Adds a Command to the auto chooser.
123   *
124   * <p>This is done to load autonomous commands when and only when they are selected, in order to
125   * save memory and file loading time for unused autonomous commands.
126   *
127   * <h3>Example:</h3>
128   *
129   * <pre><code>
130   * AutoChooser chooser;
131   * Autos autos = new Autos(swerve, shooter, intake, feeder);
132   * public Robot() {
133   *   chooser = new AutoChooser("/Choosers");
134   *   SmartDashboard.putData(chooser);
135   *   // fourPieceLeft is a method that accepts an AutoFactory and returns a command.
136   *   chooser.addCmd("4 Piece left", autos::fourPieceLeft);
137   *   chooser.addCmd("Just Shoot", shooter::shoot);
138   * }
139   * </code></pre>
140   *
141   * @param name The name of the autonomous command.
142   * @param generator The function that generates an autonomous command.
143   * @see AutoChooser#addRoutine
144   */
145  public void addCmd(String name, Supplier<Command> generator) {
146    autoRoutines.put(name, generator);
147    options = autoRoutines.keySet().toArray(new String[0]);
148  }
149
150  /**
151   * Gets a Command that schedules the selected auto routine. This Command finishes immediately as
152   * it simply schedules another Command. This Command can directly be bound to a trigger, like so:
153   *
154   * <pre><code>
155   *     AutoChooser chooser = ...;
156   *
157   *     public Robot() {
158   *         RobotModeTriggers.autonomous().whileTrue(chooser.selectedCommandScheduler());
159   *     }
160   * </code></pre>
161   *
162   * @return A command that runs the selected {@link AutoRoutine}
163   */
164  public Command selectedCommandScheduler() {
165    return Commands.defer(() -> selectedCommand.asProxy(), Set.of());
166  }
167
168  /**
169   * Returns the currently selected command.
170   *
171   * <p>If you plan on using this {@link Command} in a {@code Trigger} it is recommended to use
172   * {@link #selectedCommandScheduler()} instead.
173   *
174   * @return The currently selected command.
175   */
176  public Command selectedCommand() {
177    return selectedCommand.withName(activeCommandName);
178  }
179
180  @Override
181  public void initSendable(SendableBuilder builder) {
182    builder.setSmartDashboardType("String Chooser");
183    builder.publishConstBoolean(".controllable", true);
184    builder.publishConstString("default", NONE_NAME);
185    builder.addStringArrayProperty("options", () -> options, null);
186    builder.addStringProperty("selected", () -> selected, this::select);
187    builder.addStringProperty("active", () -> select(selected), null);
188  }
189}
